row_verdicts.required_evidence: full drag shift cmd and scope-selection proof across required surfaces
row_verdicts.classification_note: Closed with evidence on staging build build-mmz7p245 via the isolated selection packet covering queue Shift-range, queue Cmd discontiguous selection, queue Cmd+A scope selection, and document Shift-range proof.
row_verdicts.next_action: Closed with evidence on the current staging build.

row_verdicts.classification_note: Accepted limitation after remediation: the Orders document grid now validates pasted numeric values before writeback, rejects invalid manual sample edits, and batches document `onChange` once per paste via `onPasteEnd`, but a separate live autosave round-trip proof for large spreadsheet edits has still not been captured.
row_verdicts.next_action: Keep the limitation attached until a fresh live probe captures the autosave round-trip on a deployed build.

row_verdicts.classification_note: Accepted limitation: approved-field rectangular paste is proven in unit tests and prior staging keyboard paste evidence, but browser clipboard API readback remains environment-dependent and is not the acceptance signal for this row.
row_verdicts.next_action: Keep the limitation attached until a fresh live probe captures the current deployed paste path without relying on clipboard readback.

row_verdicts.classification_note: Closed with evidence on staging build build-mmxxcgce via the shipped-build narrow fill-handle probe; keep the no-reload persistence caveat attached.
row_verdicts.review_note: Claude did not reopen the row, but noted the probe proves live propagation on the route and build rather than a separate post-reload persistence round-trip.

row_verdicts.required_evidence: clear-style actions and structured edit rejection proof
row_verdicts.classification_note: Code-proven via unit tests: positive-path tests prove Delete and Ctrl+X are not suppressed on approved editable fields (quantity, cogsPerUnit), and onCellSelectionDeleteStart passes through without rejection. Negative-path tests prove locked-column cut, delete, and fill suppression with structured rejection messages.
row_verdicts.next_action: Row is code-proven. Live staging confirmation deferred until fresh build is available.

row_verdicts.required_evidence: Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Shift+Enter, and Escape proof on staging
row_verdicts.classification_note: Already safe to treat as closed with direct staging evidence from the broad G2 packet.

Limitation: the Orders document grid deliberately sets sortable: false and filter: false on every column. The focusedRowId targeting fix is implemented and unit-tested but cannot be exercised on the live Orders surface because no sort/filter controls exist. This is a surface-design constraint, not a code gap. Per the PowersheetGrid boundary contract, no other module may claim ORD-SS-012 coverage from Orders until a live sort/filter-capable surface proves the invariant.
row_verdicts.review_note: Limitation packet published. Row no longer blocks G2 on speculative proof work.

row_verdicts.required_evidence: duplicate, quick-add, and delete row-operation proof on staging
row_verdicts.classification_note: Already safe to treat as closed with direct staging evidence from the broad G2 packet.

row_verdicts.required_evidence: failure-mode bundle proof beyond immediate invalid-edit rejection
row_verdicts.classification_note: Code-proven via unit tests covering the full failure-mode bundle: fill-end validation revert (invalid quantity reverts to snapshot), invalid cogsPerUnit rejection with revert, invalid clipboard paste blocking, paste-rectangle overflow into locked columns, locked-column cut/delete/fill suppression, and hidden-row preservation during fill writeback. Undo-across-autosave sub-requirement deferred as a limitation (requires end-to-end integration).
row_verdicts.next_action: Row is code-proven. Undo-across-autosave requires future end-to-end integration testing.

next_move.summary: TER-795 is closed with evidence. Keep G2 sealed and follow the reopened G6 rollout verdict for the remaining retirement remediation work.
next_move.command_hint: Use `docs/specs/spreadsheet-native-foundation/orders-runtime/G6-rollout-verdict.md` as the active closure lane; do not spend more TER-795 row budget unless a regression reopens G2.
next_move.cadence_rule: Each writable tranche ends in exactly one of: user-facing product change, closure packet, limitation packet, or blocker packet.

current_truth: The shared runtime seam already exists in `PowersheetGrid`, and the March 18 targeted runtime tests proved the current adapter path is real rather than aspirational.
`TER-794`, `TER-795`, and `TER-796` are currently safe to treat as closed with evidence; all 9 G2 rows are classified.
Staging build `build-mmz7p245` on route `/sales?tab=orders&surface=sheet-native&orderId=627` is the latest shipped reference build used for live G2 selection evidence.
The repaired selection probe proves queue Shift-range, queue Cmd discontiguous selection, queue Cmd+A scope selection, and document Shift-range behavior on the live Orders surfaces.
`SALE-ORD-019` is closed with evidence via `output/playwright/orders-runtime-g2/2026-03-20/orders-runtime-selection-closure-packet.json` on build `build-mmz7p245`.

The fill tranche remains closed: staging build `build-mmxxcgce` recorded `3,4 -> 5,6` on the isolated live fill-handle probe with no license warnings or page errors, so `SALE-ORD-022` stays closed with evidence.
`SALE-ORD-031` is now a published limitation: Orders document grid disables sort/filter by design, so ORD-SS-012 cannot be proved on the originating surface, but that no longer blocks G2.
`SALE-ORD-029` and `SALE-ORD-035` are code-proven with comprehensive unit test coverage of positive and negative paths.

`SALE-ORD-020` and `SALE-ORD-021` are accepted limitations: the repo now batches paste writeback and hardens invalid-value rejection, but the remaining live evidence gap is documented instead of hidden behind blocker language.
All 9 G2 rows are now classified: 4 live-proven, 2 code-proven, and 3 limitation-backed.
`G2`, `G3`, and `G4` are closed with evidence. The overall Orders runtime initiative is now executing against reopened `G6` retirement remediation.

proof_budget.local_proofability: green locally and closed with shipped-build evidence for SALE-ORD-019 and SALE-ORD-022
proof_budget.rule: Do not spend more TER-795 live proof budget unless a new regression reopens G2. Keep TER-796 sealed, preserve the SALE-ORD-022 no-reload caveat, and use G6-specific proof artifacts for the active retirement-remediation gate.

repair_queue: All 9 G2 rows are now classified. No TER-795 rows remain in the active repair queue.
Keep `TER-796` sealed unless a future isolated row-op rerun reproduces a real regression.
Keep the `SALE-ORD-022` closure packet honest: it proves shipped-route propagation on build `build-mmxxcgce`, not a separate reload or persistence round-trip.
Keep `SALE-ORD-031` recorded as a limitation until a live Orders surface actually exercises sort/filter.
Active execution has moved to reopened G6 retirement remediation; do not reopen G2 unless a classified row regresses.

acceptance_rows.acceptance: full drag shift cmd and scope-selection proof across required surfaces
deployed-build proof that quantity fill propagates `3,4 -> 5,6` or an explicit limitation packet
clear-style actions and structured edit rejection proof
failure-mode bundle proof beyond immediate invalid-edit rejection

prior_reviews.conclusion: `SALE-ORD-022` could not be promoted before ship; `SALE-ORD-031` should stay partial because the live Orders document surface still disables sort/filter.
`SALE-ORD-022` remains acceptable as closed with evidence, with one residual caution that the probe proves live propagation rather than a separate reload or persistence round-trip.
